name: Uriel
description: Uriel, whose name translates to "God is my light" or "Fire of God," is a prominent archangel within Jewish and Christian apocryphal and mystical traditions. He is often depicted as one of the four major archangels, alongside Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael, although his inclusion in this quartet varies depending on the specific tradition. Uriel's role is multifaceted, encompassing wisdom, prophecy, divine fire, and judgment. He is considered an interpreter of prophecies, guiding individuals through complex divine messages and illuminating hidden truths. In some accounts, Uriel is associated with the sun and the stars, embodying the celestial fire that brings enlightenment. He is also linked to the role of divine judge, responsible for overseeing the righteous and punishing the wicked. Uriel's presence is less pronounced in canonical scriptures, lending him a greater degree of interpretive flexibility within esoteric and folk religious contexts. His continued relevance underscores the enduring human quest for divine illumination and understanding.
subtitle: Angel of Light

keyMyths.title: Uriel and Noah's Flood
keyMyths.description: According to some apocryphal traditions, Uriel warned Noah about the impending flood, allowing him time to build the Ark and save humanity from destruction. Uriel's role in this event highlights his connection to divine judgment and his willingness to intercede on behalf of humanity. This intervention showcases Uriel as a protective figure, ensuring the continuation of God's plan despite humanity's transgressions. The warning provided by Uriel allowed Noah to prepare for the cataclysm, demonstrating the archangel's foresight and divine knowledge.

keyMyths.title: Uriel and Ezra's Questions
keyMyths.description: In 2 Esdras, Uriel is sent by God to answer Ezra's profound questions about the nature of justice, suffering, and the fate of humanity. Uriel's responses, though often enigmatic, challenge Ezra to contemplate the limitations of human understanding in the face of divine mysteries. This interaction emphasizes the role of Uriel as a guide and teacher, leading individuals towards deeper spiritual insights, even if complete comprehension remains elusive. The dialogue between Ezra and Uriel explores the complexities of faith and the problem of evil.

keyMyths.title: Uriel Guarding the Gates of Paradise
keyMyths.description: Some traditions place Uriel as the guardian of the gates of Paradise or Eden, wielding a fiery sword to prevent the unworthy from entering. This role reinforces Uriel's association with divine judgment and his role in maintaining the sanctity of sacred spaces. The fiery sword symbolizes the divine power and authority entrusted to him, safeguarding the purity of Paradise from those who are not spiritually prepared to enter. This imagery highlights the exclusive nature of divine grace and the need for spiritual purification.

sources.author: Davidson, Gustav
sources.work: A Dictionary of Angels, Including the Fallen Angels
sources.description: A comprehensive dictionary of angels, providing detailed information on Uriel's various roles and associations.
sources.text: A comprehensive dictionary of angels, providing detailed information on Uriel's various roles and associations.

sources.text: A critical edition of the Book of Enoch, which contains significant references to Uriel and his prophetic functions.

associations.name: Red
associations.significance: Represents divine fire, passion, and zeal in serving God.

associations.name: Fire
associations.significance: Symbolizes purification, enlightenment, and the illuminating power of divine wisdom.

associations.name: Prophecy
associations.significance: Uriel is a primary source of prophetic knowledge and insight into the future.

associations.name: Sun
associations.significance: Represents divine illumination and the dispelling of darkness through knowledge.

associations.name: Four
associations.significance: Often associated with the four major archangels and the four corners of the Earth, symbolizing completeness and universality.

associations.name: Wisdom
associations.significance: Embodies divine wisdom and understanding, guiding humanity towards enlightenment.

worshipPractices.name: Invocation
worshipPractices.description: Prayers and invocations are offered to Uriel for guidance, wisdom, and understanding, especially in times of confusion or seeking divine knowledge. These often involve candles and meditative practices.

festivals.name: Michaelmas
festivals.description: While primarily dedicated to Archangel Michael, Uriel is sometimes included in Michaelmas celebrations, particularly in churches that recognize him as a major archangel. Prayers and hymns may be offered in his honor.

cultural.modernLegacy: Uriel's influence is seen in modern esoteric and New Age spiritual practices, where he is invoked for enlightenment and problem-solving. He is also a popular figure in angel therapy and contemporary art, representing wisdom and divine fire.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Uriel's origins are rooted in Jewish apocryphal and mystical literature, particularly the Book of Enoch and other Second Temple texts. Unlike Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael, Uriel is not explicitly named in the canonical Hebrew Bible or Christian Old Testament. However, his presence and role gradually solidified within Jewish angelic hierarchies and later Christian traditions. The name 'Uriel' itself, meaning 'God is my light' or 'Fire of God,' suggests a divine essence and purpose closely tied to illumination and revelation. Early texts depict him as one of the angels present at creation, tasked with overseeing the cosmos and guiding humanity. The development of Uriel's character reflects an evolving understanding of angelic roles and their interaction with the mortal world, bridging the gap between the divine and human realms through wisdom and prophetic insight.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: One of Uriel's prominent roles is in the Book of Enoch, where he serves as a guide, interpreting visions and revealing cosmic secrets to Enoch. He is also depicted as warning Noah about the impending flood, instructing him on how to build the Ark and save humanity. In Christian traditions, Uriel is sometimes identified as the angel who guarded the gates of Eden after Adam and Eve were expelled, wielding a fiery sword. Another story recounts Uriel's role in interpreting prophecies for the prophet Ezra. These narratives consistently portray Uriel as a source of divine knowledge, offering guidance and understanding to those who seek it. His involvement in pivotal moments in biblical history underscores his significance as a messenger of God and a guardian of sacred knowledge, illuminating the path for humanity amidst trials and tribulations.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Uriel's legacy extends beyond religious texts into art, literature, and contemporary spirituality. In John Milton's 'Paradise Lost,' Uriel is depicted as the angel closest to the sun, possessing keen sight and serving as a guide to Satan (who deceives him). This portrayal highlights Uriel's association with light and knowledge, but also his vulnerability to deception. In modern angelology, Uriel is often invoked for clarity, problem-solving, and academic pursuits. He is considered a patron of the arts and sciences, inspiring creativity and innovation. His influence can be seen in various forms of media, from paintings and sculptures to novels and films, where he is often depicted as a radiant figure offering wisdom and guidance. Uriel's enduring appeal lies in his promise of enlightenment and understanding in a world often shrouded in darkness and uncertainty.

shortDescription: Uriel, meaning "God is my light", is an archangel in Abrahamic traditions known for wisdom and prophecy.
